text:
Admission Date:  1976-05-22              Discharge Date:   1997-09-24

Date of Birth:  1972-09-12             Sex:   M

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:Katie
Chief Complaint:
Transient Left visual field disturbances in both eyes

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
Stent placed in Right Internal Carotid Artery


History of Present Illness:
61 year old male with history of CABG in 06-16 who developed
intermittent L upper quadrant visual field disturbances in both
eyes in the weeks and months following his bypass surgery.
Carotid Ultrasound revealed severe stenosis in both [**Country **] and L
Carotid bulb. He was admitted electively for [**Country **] stent

Past Medical History:
1. CAD s/p CABG in 06-16
2. HTN
3. Hyperlipidemia

Social History:
Married with 3 children. Works on farm. Quit smoking last year
when had MI/CABG. Drinks approx 4 vodkas per day. No IVDU

Family History:
Father with MI at 46.

Physical Exam:
Afebrile 126/62 57 12 99% on RA
Sitting up in bed post procedure. Alert and Oriented x3 in NAD
PERRL. Anicteric. OP clear with MMM
Neck supple. No JVD. L Carotid bruit but 2+ upstrokes
RRR. Nl S1 and S2. 2/6 systolic murmurs at LUSB and LLSB
radiating to axilla
CTAB
Soft. Overweight. NTND. No masses. No HSM. Normal BS
R groin without hematoma or bruit
Extremeties without C/C/E
No facial assymmetry. Moving all extremeties symmetrically.

Pertinent Results:
No labs.
Cath showed bilateral carotid disease with good flow following
[**Country **] stenting

Brief Hospital Course:
Patient underwent successful [**Country **]. He was monitored in CCU
overnight post procedure and had no neurologic or hemodynamic
events. He was restarted on his outpatient medications and
discharged with instructions to follow up with Dr. Heather and
Dr. Wilson

Medications on Admission:
1. Aspirin 325 mg Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) Sig: One (1)
Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) PO DAILY (Daily).
2. Clopidogrel Bisulfate 75 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO
DAILY (Daily) for 30 days.
3. Atenolol 50 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily).
4. Lisinopril 10 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily).

5. Hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg Tablet Sig: 0.5 Tablet PO DAILY
(Daily).
6. Atorvastatin Calcium 40 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO
DAILY (Daily).

Discharge Medications:
1. Aspirin 325 mg Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) Sig: One (1)
Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) PO DAILY (Daily).
2. Clopidogrel Bisulfate 75 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO
DAILY (Daily) for life
3. Atenolol 50 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily).
Start morning of 2003-12-15
4. Lisinopril 10 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily).
 Start morning of 2003-12-15
5. Hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg Tablet Sig: 0.5 Tablet PO DAILY
(Daily). Start morning of 2003-12-15
(The 2 medications listed above (Lisinopril and
Hydrochlorothiazide together are called Zestoretic)
6. Atorvastatin Calcium 40 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO
DAILY (Daily).


Discharge Disposition:
Home

Discharge Diagnosis:
Carotid Plaque and stent to the Right Internal Carotid Artery


Discharge Condition:
Good

Discharge Instructions:
Take all your medications as directed


Followup Instructions:
1. You have an appointment this Friday with Dr. Wilson to have
your blood pressure checked

2. Call Dr. Heather to schedule an appointment in 2 months. You
will need another Carotid ultrasound before seeing him so please
call his office next week to schedule these.
